narrative: The Aurora-1 demonstration flight proceeded from the leased pad despite the flight safety analysis still being in draft and the expected casualty estimate sitting at 2.1e-4, above the regulatory ceiling but accepted by the program manager under an undocumented range safety waiver. The flight termination system had not completed environmental qualification, and the autonomous termination logic was single-string. CAD drawings and trajectory files were emailed to a subcontracted analytics firm in Singapore without a Technical Assistance Agreement, and no CCATS export classification was on file for the foreign-supplied avionics. Hazard area clearance relied on a verbal call to the harbormaster rather than a published NOTMAR. Propellant loading was performed by a single technician after the second operator went off-shift. The upper stage was left in a 1140 km orbit with no disposal maneuver planned, exceeding the 25-year LEO removal expectation by an order of magnitude.
